verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1 / TMP21) is a type I transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family that cycles between the ER and Golgi as COPI/COPII cargo, functioning as a bulk-flow transmembrane cargo receptor in the early secretory pathway. Its steady-state residence is in the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and cis-Golgi, with no documented stable pool at the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Checking contextual buckets: (1) no documented cancer ecto-pool for TMED10 protein body; (2) no documented cell-death-induced PM exposure; (3) no germline/developmental outer-leaflet display; (4) no activation-induced surface translocation; (5) no lysosomal exocytosis route; (6) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to cell surface. It has a TM domain but is an endomembrane-resident protein, not a plasma membrane receptor. The protein body does not reach the outer face of the PM under any documented condition.
